evals.expected_output: A structured proposal covering: (1) a semantic token table, (2) contrast ratio check on the button, (3) component candidates with props and file paths, (4) ordered migration plan. No code written.

assertions.name: proposal-not-code
assertions.description: Output is a proposal/plan, not actual implementation code or file edits

assertions.name: token-table-present
assertions.description: Proposal contains a table or list of semantic tokens with names, values, and roles

assertions.name: contrast-ratio-mentioned
assertions.description: Proposal mentions WCAG AA contrast ratio check for the primary button

assertions.name: component-candidate-listed
assertions.description: Proposal identifies the nav link block as a component extraction candidate with props

assertions.name: migration-order-given
assertions.description: Proposal includes an ordered migration plan

evals.prompt:
hey can you look at my tailwind project and find all the places where I'm hardcoding colors instead of using css variables? I want to set up a proper token system.

Here's what I'm seeing in my components:
```tsx
// Button.tsx
<button className="bg-[#4fb8b2] text-white hover:bg-[#328f97]">

// Card.tsx  
<div className="border border-[rgba(23,58,64,0.14)] bg-white/80">

// Nav.tsx
<a className="text-[#416166] hover:text-[#173a40]">
```

My styles.css has some variables but I'm not using them consistently.
evals.expected_output: An audit identifying the hardcoded hex values, followed by a proposal showing how to centralize them with semantic CSS variable names. Should not write any files.

assertions.name: identifies-hardcoded-values
assertions.description: Identifies the specific hardcoded hex/rgba values from the snippets

assertions.name: proposes-specific-token-names
assertions.description: Proposes specific CSS variable names (e.g. --color-primary, --color-border) not just 'use variables'

assertions.name: maps-values-to-tokens
assertions.description: Shows which hardcoded value maps to which proposed token

assertions.name: no-code-written
assertions.description: Does not write or edit any files — only proposes changes

evals.prompt:
My React app has a ton of UI duplication. Every page has this same pattern at the top: ```tsx // contacts.tsx, journal.tsx, reminders.tsx, settings.tsx — all have:

People

Contacts

``` And every modal is a hand-rolled overlay: ```tsx
...
``` I want to extract shared components but need a plan first.
evals.expected_output: A component extraction proposal naming PageHeader and Modal/Dialog as candidates, with prop interfaces defined and instance counts noted. Should recommend extraction order.

assertions.name: names-pageheader-component
assertions.description: Proposes a PageHeader (or equivalent) component for the kicker+title+action pattern

assertions.name: names-modal-component
assertions.description: Proposes a Modal or Dialog component for the hand-rolled overlay pattern

assertions.name: proposes-prop-interfaces
assertions.description: Defines specific props for at least one proposed component (e.g. kicker, title, action)

assertions.name: quantifies-duplication
assertions.description: Notes how many files/instances the pattern appears in

assertions.name: extraction-order-given
assertions.description: Recommends an order for extraction

evals.expected_output: A styled-primitive extraction proposal for a Button component using cva, with variants (default/outline/ghost/destructive) and sizes (default/icon) defined, headless primitive chosen, and a migration plan covering all call sites. No code written yet.

assertions.name: identifies-button-as-styled-primitive
assertions.description: Recognizes the duplication as a styled primitive (variant system) rather than a structural JSX copy-paste

assertions.name: proposes-cva-variants
assertions.description: Proposes using cva (class-variance-authority) with specific named variants matching the patterns shown

assertions.name: variant-names-match
assertions.description: Proposed variant names cover at least: default/primary, outline, ghost, destructive

assertions.name: mentions-headless-primitive
assertions.description: Recommends using the existing @base-ui/react or similar headless primitive rather than a raw <button>

assertions.name: no-code-written
assertions.description: Does not write or edit any files — only proposes the extraction

assertions.name: migration-plan-given
assertions.description: Includes a migration plan or call-site count for replacing the inline className strings
